pattern: A bitmask DP over which elements of the second array are already used finds the minimum-cost perfect matching between the two arrays.
hints: This is an assignment problem: match each nums1 element to a distinct nums2 element minimizing the total XOR.
With at most fourteen elements, track the subset of nums2 already assigned with a bitmask.
Let dp[mask] be the best cost using the popcount(mask) first elements of nums1 against the chosen subset; extend by trying each unused bit.

edge_inputs.description: zeros pair with zeros for zero cost

stress.description: random equal-length pairs up to length nine to keep the exponential oracle fast
